content:
The idea that lifting your arms above your head during pregnancy will harm the baby is actually a myth. There is no medical evidence to support the claim that raising your arms above your head causes any harm to the baby.

In fact, lifting your arms above your head during pregnancy is generally safe. It might sometimes cause temporary discomfort or lightheadedness in some pregnant people, especially if done quickly or if they are standing for a long time, due to changes in blood circulation. However, this is not harmful to the baby.

If you ever feel dizzy, uncomfortable, or short of breath while moving or exercising during pregnancy, it’s a good idea to stop, rest, and talk to your healthcare provider. But simply raising your arms above your head is not something you need to avoid for the baby’s safety.

If you have any specific concerns or symptoms, consulting with a healthcare professional is always the best course of action.
